Test Description: Built the initial admin page and checked a regular customer could not access it
Input: Logged in as a customer and typed admin.php into the address bar
Expected Outcome: 403 access denied

Test Description: Opened the overview section for the first time to see if the stats and chart rendered
Input: Logged in as admin and loaded the page
Expected Outcome: KPI cards revenue chart recent orders and low stock all visible

Test Description: Noticed the total revenue figure looked too high and checked if cancelled orders were being counted
Input: Cancelled a paid order and reloaded the overview
Expected Outcome: Revenue should drop by that order amount
Actual Outcome: Revenue did not change — cancelled orders were included in the total
Notes: Added WHERE status != cancelled to the revenue query

Test Description: Cancelled another order and reloaded to confirm the fix
Input: Cancelled an order
Expected Outcome: Revenue drops correctly

Test Description: Realised the monthly revenue stat might be pulling in orders from the same month in previous years
Input: Inserted a test order dated last year with the same month number
Expected Outcome: Only orders from the current year and month should count
Actual Outcome: Previous year order was being included because the query filtered by month only
Notes: Added a year check alongside the month check in the query

Test Description: Reloaded the monthly stat with the old test order still in the database
Input: Old order in database
Expected Outcome: Prior year order excluded

Test Description: Scrolled down to check the 30-day revenue line chart rendered with real data
Input: Viewed the overview after a few orders had been placed
Expected Outcome: Line chart showing daily revenue for the last 30 days

Test Description: Built the orders section and loaded it to see if the list came through
Input: Opened admin.php?section=orders
Expected Outcome: All orders listed with customer name total and status

Test Description: Tried the status filter to narrow down to pending orders only
Input: Selected Pending from the dropdown and applied
Expected Outcome: Only pending orders shown

Test Description: Typed a partial customer name in the search box to check it filtered correctly
Input: Partial name entered
Expected Outcome: Only matching orders returned

Test Description: Tried searching using an email address typed in uppercase and got no results
Input: Typed the email in uppercase
Expected Outcome: Should find the same orders regardless of case
Actual Outcome: No results returned — search was case sensitive
Notes: Used LOWER() on both sides of the LIKE comparison

Test Description: Repeated the uppercase email search after the fix
Input: Uppercase email in the search
Expected Outcome: Matching orders returned

Test Description: Opened an order that was already dispatched and noticed the status dropdown defaulted to pending instead of showing the current status
Input: Opened a dispatched order
Expected Outcome: Dropdown should show dispatched selected
Actual Outcome: Always showed pending regardless of the order status
Notes: Added the selected attribute to the option that matches the current order status

Test Description: Reopened the dispatched order after the fix
Input: Order with dispatched status
Expected Outcome: Dropdown shows dispatched pre-selected

Test Description: Changed an order from pending to paid and checked a log entry was created
Input: Selected Paid and clicked Save
Expected Outcome: Status updated and a log row in order_status_log

Test Description: Cancelled an order and checked the product stock went back up
Input: Changed a pending order to cancelled
Expected Outcome: Stock for each ordered item restored

Test Description: Built the revenue by product report and noticed it was sorted alphabetically instead of by revenue
Input: Viewed the products report tab
Expected Outcome: Highest earning product at the top
Notes: Added ORDER BY revenue DESC to the report query

Test Description: Reloaded the product report after the sort fix
Input: Opened the products report
Expected Outcome: Highest revenue first

Test Description: Spotted that a product with no orders was missing from the report entirely
Input: Product with no orders in the database
Expected Outcome: Should appear with 0 units sold and £0.00 revenue
Actual Outcome: Was not in the list at all
Notes: Changed INNER JOIN to LEFT JOIN so products without any orders still appear

Test Description: Reloaded the report after switching to a left join
Input: Viewed report
Expected Outcome: Zero-order product now visible with zeros

Test Description: Opened the revenue over time tab for the first time
Input: Clicked the timeline tab
Expected Outcome: Chart and day-by-day revenue table

Test Description: Set a custom from and to date and applied the filter
Input: Date range entered
Expected Outcome: Only data within that range shown

Test Description: Typed a new stock number into the inventory table and saved it
Input: Updated a product stock value and clicked Save
Expected Outcome: Stock updated in the database

Test Description: Tried typing a negative number into the stock field to see what happened
Input: Entered -5 as the stock value
Expected Outcome: Should save as 0 not a negative

Test Description: Opened the customers section to see the registered accounts
Input: Navigated to admin.php?section=customers
Expected Outcome: All customers listed with order count and total spend

Test Description: Edited a customer name and email through the admin panel to check it saved
Input: Changed both fields and submitted
Expected Outcome: Changes saved with a flash message

Test Description: Went through all POST forms and deliberately sent requests with wrong CSRF tokens to check they were all rejected
Input: Forged POST requests with incorrect tokens
Expected Outcome: All forms reject the request

Test Description: Reviewed every echo statement across the codebase to make sure nothing was printed raw
Input: Read through all PHP files
Expected Outcome: All output should go through the e() helper

Test Description: Checked every database query to make sure nothing had user input directly in the SQL string
Input: Reviewed all queries
Expected Outcome: All should use prepare and bind_param

Test Description: Noted the session ID before login and compared it after to check for session fixation
Input: Captured the cookie value before and after logging in
Expected Outcome: Session ID should change on login

Test Description: Ran a full customer journey start to finish to check everything worked together
Input: Registered an account browsed products added to cart checked out and viewed the order in account history
Expected Outcome: Every step should work and the order appears in account history

Test Description: Ran a full admin workflow to check the order management loop worked
Input: Logged in as admin found a new pending order marked it paid and checked the status log
Expected Outcome: Status updated and log entry recorded

Test Description: Compared product cards side by side on home and main and noticed portrait phone images made their cards much taller than landscape laptop cards
Input: Opened both pages and compared card heights visually
Expected Outcome: All cards should be the same height
Actual Outcome: Card heights were inconsistent because images had no fixed container
Notes: Wrapped images in a fixed-height container with overflow hidden so all cards are the same height regardless of image orientation

Test Description: Reopened both pages after the container fix
Input: Viewed home and main pages
Expected Outcome: Uniform card heights

Test Description: Looked at the phone card to check portrait images filled the container without the subject being cut off badly
Input: Opened home.php and examined the phone card
Expected Outcome: Image fills the container with the subject visible
Actual Outcome: Cropped sensibly with object-position top keeping the top of the image in view

Test Description: Noticed laptop images were being cropped on the left and right sides because object-fit cover was applied to landscape images the same as portrait
Input: Opened main.php and looked at the laptop cards
Expected Outcome: Landscape images should fill the full width
Actual Outcome: Sides of landscape images were being cropped
Notes: Added orientation detection using getimagesize so landscape images get object-fit fill instead of cover

Test Description: Reloaded main.php after the orientation detection was added
Input: Opened main.php
Expected Outcome: Landscape images fill without cropping

Test Description: Looked at the home page cards and thought the images were too small to really showcase the products
Input: Opened home.php and measured the image container
Expected Outcome: Images should be large and clear
Actual Outcome: Images were only 120px wide with height auto so they looked tiny and uneven across cards
Notes: Increased the image container to 200x200px with object-fit cover

Test Description: Reloaded after increasing the container size
Input: Opened home.php
Expected Outcome: Larger images consistent across all cards

Test Description: Noticed the top of tall portrait images was being cut off on cards and the product detail page
Input: Checked cards on all three pages
Expected Outcome: Top of the image should always be visible
Actual Outcome: object-position center was cutting the top of portrait images
Notes: Changed to object-position top on all image containers

Test Description: Checked all cards and the product page after the object-position fix
Input: Opened cards and product pages
Expected Outcome: Top of each portrait image preserved

Test Description: Opened the site on a phone-sized screen in DevTools and found the nav links fell off the edge and were unreachable
Input: DevTools set to 375px width
Expected Outcome: Nav should be usable on mobile
Notes: Added a hamburger button shown below 640px that toggles the nav links open and closed with a CSS class

Test Description: Rechecked on the same screen size after adding the hamburger
Input: 375px in DevTools
Expected Outcome: Hamburger visible and links show when tapped

Test Description: Landed on the order success page after a test checkout and noticed the price shown was just the raw total with no VAT breakdown
Input: Completed a test checkout
Expected Outcome: Subtotal VAT and grand total should all be shown separately
Actual Outcome: Only the raw total was displayed
Notes: Added VAT calculation and split the invoice preview table into subtotal VAT at 20% and grand total rows

Test Description: Completed another checkout after the fix
Input: Went through checkout
Expected Outcome: Subtotal VAT and grand total shown

Test Description: Built the dedicated invoice page and opened it as the order owner
Input: Opened invoice.php with a valid order ID
Expected Outcome: Full invoice with customer details delivery address line items VAT and total

Test Description: Tried loading a different customer's invoice while logged in as another user
Input: Used another customer's order ID in the URL
Expected Outcome: 403 access denied

Test Description: Checked admin could open any customer's invoice for support purposes
Input: Logged in as admin and opened a customer invoice
Expected Outcome: Should load with no access error

Test Description: Realised there was no way for the customer to reach the invoice page from their account — no link existed
Input: Inspected the order history on account.php
Expected Outcome: Each order row should have a View Invoice link
Actual Outcome: Link was not there at all
Notes: Added the anchor tag linking to invoice.php in the order header row

Test Description: Clicked the View Invoice link after adding it
Input: Clicked the link on an order
Expected Outcome: Correct invoice page opens

Test Description: Changed an order to arrived in the admin panel then opened account.php and the badge was missing for that status
Input: Set an order to arrived and opened account.php
Expected Outcome: Green Arrived badge should appear
Actual Outcome: Badge was missing because arrived was not in the status colour map
Notes: Added arrived to the statusBadge map with the same green used for paid

Test Description: Reloaded account.php after adding arrived to the badge map
Input: Set an order to arrived
Expected Outcome: Green Arrived badge shown

Test Description: Checked that the Leave a Review link only appeared next to arrived orders and not others
Input: Viewed account.php with orders at different statuses
Expected Outcome: Link only beside arrived orders

Test Description: Built the review page and opened it as the customer who owned an arrived order
Input: Opened review.php with a valid arrived order ID
Expected Outcome: Each product in the order shown with a star rating and comment box

Test Description: Tried opening review.php with an order that had status paid to check it was blocked
Input: Opened with a paid order ID
Expected Outcome: Should reject with an error

Test Description: Submitted a review with 4 stars and a comment to check it saved and showed a success message
Input: Chose 4 stars wrote a comment and submitted
Expected Outcome: Review in database and success message shown

Test Description: Submitted the same review again to see if a duplicate would be stored
Input: Submitted a review then tried again on the same order
Expected Outcome: Second submission should be silently skipped
Actual Outcome: Ignored with the already reviewed note shown
Notes: Uses INSERT IGNORE

Test Description: Partially reviewed an order then reopened the page to check the already reviewed note appeared on the done products
Input: Opened review.php for a partially reviewed order
Expected Outcome: Form hidden and note shown for reviewed items
Actual Outcome: Note showed and form hidden correctly

Test Description: Submitted a review and went to that product page expecting to see it — there was no reviews section at all
Input: Submitted a review then opened the product page
Expected Outcome: Reviews section below the viewer with name rating and comment
Notes: Added the reviews query and the HTML reviews section to the bottom of product.php

Test Description: Reopened the product page after adding the reviews section
Input: Submitted a review and reloaded the product
Expected Outcome: Reviews section with all details visible

Test Description: Added two reviews with ratings 3 and 5 and checked the average displayed correctly
Input: Two reviews rated 3 and 5 on the same product
Expected Outcome: Average shows as 4.0 with star icons

Test Description: Opened a product that had no reviews to check the empty state message appeared
Input: Opened a product with no reviews
Expected Outcome: No reviews yet message visible

Test Description: Changed an order to arrived expecting the customer to receive a review invitation email but nothing arrived
Input: Set status to arrived
Expected Outcome: Customer should receive an email with a review link
Actual Outcome: No email — the order had no customer_email stored because the account email was not being used as a fallback
Notes: Joined the users table in the query to fall back to the account email when customer_email is empty

Test Description: Changed another order to arrived after the email fallback fix
Input: Set status to arrived
Expected Outcome: Email received by the customer

Test Description: Checked the sent email inbox and found the email went to the admin address not the customer
Input: Changed another order to arrived and checked both inboxes
Expected Outcome: Email should land in the customer inbox
Actual Outcome: Delivered to the admin inbox because the To Email field in the EmailJS template was hardcoded to the admin address
Notes: Changed the To Email field in the EmailJS template to use the dynamic to_email variable passed in the template params

Test Description: Changed another order to arrived after fixing the template
Input: Set status to arrived
Expected Outcome: Email in the customer inbox

Test Description: Clicked the review link in the email and got a 404
Input: Opened the link from the customer email
Expected Outcome: Review page should open
Actual Outcome: 404 — the link was built using window.location.origin in JavaScript which did not include the subfolder path on the server
Notes: Changed to a server-built URL using PHP so the full path including the subfolder is always correct

Test Description: Clicked the link again after fixing how the URL was built
Input: Changed another order to arrived and opened the email link
Expected Outcome: Review page loads

Test Description: Stayed on the orders page after changing an order to arrived and the email only arrived once I navigated away to another section
Input: Changed to arrived and stayed put
Expected Outcome: Email should arrive straight away
Actual Outcome: Email only sent when the next page loaded because the EmailJS send was triggered by a client-side script on the redirected page
Notes: Switched to a server-side PHP cURL call to the EmailJS API so the email fires during the POST handler before the redirect

Test Description: Changed another order to arrived after the cURL fix
Input: Set status to arrived
Expected Outcome: Email arrives without needing to navigate away

Test Description: Tried opening review.php as admin to test the review flow and got access denied because the admin did not own the order
Input: Opened review.php while logged in as admin
Expected Outcome: Page should load for admin
Actual Outcome: Access denied — admin ownership check failed
Notes: Added is_admin() to the access check so admins bypass the ownership requirement

Test Description: Opened the review page as admin after the fix
Input: Logged in as admin and opened review.php
Expected Outcome: Page loads

Test Description: Clicked a review link while logged out and after logging in was sent to home.php instead of back to the review page
Input: Opened a review link while not logged in then logged in
Expected Outcome: Should redirect back to review.php after login
Actual Outcome: Went to home.php — review.php was not in the allowed redirect list
Notes: Added review.php to the allowedNext array in login.php

Test Description: Clicked the review link while logged out and logged in after the fix
Input: Not logged in opened a review link then logged in
Expected Outcome: Lands on the review page

Test Description: Set a product stock to 0 and ordered it — the order went through and stock dropped to -1
Input: Set stock to 0 then added the product and checked out
Expected Outcome: Order should be blocked with an error
Actual Outcome: Order placed successfully and stock went negative — no stock check existed
Notes: Added a stock check to the add action in processor.php to block adding 0-stock items to the cart and a second check before the checkout transaction to block the order if any item has no stock

Test Description: Set stock to 0 again and tried to add the item to the cart
Input: Set stock to 0 then clicked Add to cart
Expected Outcome: Flash error and item not added
Actual Outcome: Error appeared and cart was unchanged

Test Description: A user pointed out they could not see where to fill in their address — the fields were hidden and only appeared after typing a name
Input: Opened the cart with items and looked at the form before typing anything
Expected Outcome: All delivery fields should be visible straight away
Actual Outcome: Address block was hidden by a JavaScript toggle that waited for a name to be entered first
Notes: Removed the JS toggle function and the display none inline style so all fields show immediately when the cart is opened

Test Description: Opened the cart after removing the toggle
Input: Cart with items in session
Expected Outcome: All fields visible without any interaction

Test Description: Tried to add a brand new product through the admin panel — there was no form for it
Input: Looked through the inventory section
Expected Outcome: Should have an add product form with all fields
Actual Outcome: No way to create a product from the admin at all — would have to go direct to the database
Notes: Added an add product form at the top of the inventory section with fields for name category version price stock and description

Test Description: Filled in the add product form and submitted it
Input: Entered all required fields and clicked Add Product
Expected Outcome: New product appears in the inventory table
Actual Outcome: Product added and appeared in the list

Test Description: Tried editing all the details of an existing product not just the stock and price
Input: Clicked the new Edit button on a product row
Expected Outcome: Edit panel opens with all fields pre-filled and saveable
Actual Outcome: Edit panel opened with all fields including name category version description and price and stock

Test Description: Saved changes through the edit panel and checked the product updated
Input: Changed the name and description and clicked Save Changes
Expected Outcome: Product details updated in the database
Actual Outcome: Updated correctly and flash message shown

Test Description: Deleted a product using the new Delete button
Input: Clicked Delete on a product and confirmed the prompt
Expected Outcome: Product removed from the database and no longer in the list

Test Description: Noticed there was no way to delete a customer account through the admin
Input: Looked at the customers table
Expected Outcome: Should have a Delete button next to each customer
Actual Outcome: Only an Edit button existed — no delete option
Notes: Added a Delete button next to each customer row with a confirmation prompt

Test Description: Clicked Delete on a customer account and confirmed
Input: Clicked Delete and accepted the confirmation dialog
Expected Outcome: Customer account removed from the database
Actual Outcome: Deleted correctly and no longer in the list

Test Description: Realised there was no way to view or moderate customer reviews in the admin at all
Input: Looked through all admin sections
Expected Outcome: Should be able to see all reviews and delete any inappropriate ones
Actual Outcome: Reviews section did not exist — no visibility over what customers had submitted
Notes: Added a Reviews section to the admin nav that lists all reviews with product reviewer rating and comment and a delete button on each row

Test Description: Opened the new Reviews section and checked all reviews were listed
Input: Navigated to admin.php?section=reviews
Expected Outcome: All reviews shown with product name reviewer name star rating and comment

Test Description: Deleted a review through the admin panel
Input: Clicked Delete on a review and confirmed
Expected Outcome: Review removed from the database and no longer shown
